title: Add foundation-first partitioning procedure and learnings re-read to tick-patterns.md
description:
Implement spec §4 + §2 consumption point 2 (docs/design/execution-model-v2-epic-chaining-and-learning-loop.md) in skills/ticks/references/tick-patterns.md.

Replace the scattered partitioning guidance ('Partitioning an Epic into Ticks' section + the 'define shared contracts first' idea) with one named, ordered procedure:
0. Re-read .tick/learnings.md (if present) — partitioning and tick-authoring mistakes are a recurring learning category.
1. List every deliverable in the epic.
2. Build a work-to-file matrix: per deliverable, files created / modified.
3. Cluster by shared files — deliverables touching the same files become sequential (--blocked-by) or merge into one tick.
4. Extract the foundation: shared types, schemas, contracts, config, persistence -> wave-1 tick(s) everything else blocks on.
5. Maximize the parallel frontier after foundation; verify with tk graph that no same-wave ticks share files.

Also: add 're-read .tick/learnings.md before authoring' to the Definition of Ready preamble (the paragraph above the checklist).

Keep the existing vertical-slicing guidance and examples — the procedure incorporates them, it does not delete them. Files: skills/ticks/references/tick-patterns.md only.
acceptance_criteria: tick-patterns.md has the 6-step (0-5) partitioning procedure; DoR preamble mentions re-reading .tick/learnings.md; vertical-slicing content preserved
